description: Official turbopuffer website calculator numbers and tpuf-benchmark specs for 10M × 1024-dim vector ANN. Used as the fixed reference for openpuffer scaling comparison (not re-run without TURBOPUFFER_API_KEY).

sources.note: Vector Perf tab: 1024 dimensions, 10M docs, ~40GB; Approach (8 QPS with topk=10); links tpuf-benchmark
Infrastructure: c2-standard-30 on GCP us-central1; region gcp-us-central1
High Latency, High Throughput Writes: object-storage WAL; up to 200ms durable commit; thousands of writes/sec per namespace

infrastructure.zone_note: tpuf-benchmark README: all website benchmarks on c2-standard-30 in GCP us-central1

write_path.architecture_note: Object-storage write-ahead log; stateless nodes; documents visible to consistent reads faster than eventually-consistent search engines (tradeoffs doc).
write_path.not_comparable_to_openpuffer: openpuffer MinIO harness: WAL-limited upsert+index wait (~1 durable commit/s, docs/s ingest tiers in op-scaling-*.json). turbopuffer publishes per-write commit latency (≤200ms) and namespace write throughput (thousands/s)—different durability/throughput model; do not compare op ingest_docs_per_sec to tpuf writes_per_sec_per_namespace_claim.
write_path.note: Tradeoffs § High Latency, High Throughput Writes (2026-06-05): writes take up to 200ms to commit; system supports thousands of writes per second per namespace. Upper bound 10k/s used for structured reference (doc: 'thousands').

secondary_sources.title: ANN v3: 200ms p99 query latency over 100 billion vectors
secondary_sources.note: Fleet-scale ANN v3 engineering post (100B×1024×f16, ~200 TiB): p99≤200 ms and >1k QPS targets on SSD-backed deployments. Interactive Latency/QPS charts have no extractable (document_count, cold p50) series. Not comparable to openpuffer 100k×128 MinIO scaling (1000× fewer docs, 8× fewer dims, 7 sequential probes vs sustained QPS).

theoretical_qps_roofline_ms.note: Blog roofline tables; not measured cold p50 at 10M or 100B for calculator protocol

scaling_curve_official.note: No second official cold p50 at a different document_count was found for the Vector Perf calculator workload (1024-d, top_k=10, 8 QPS × 30m). Homepage calculator and tpuf-benchmark benchmarks/website/ are fixed at 10M docs. ANN v3 blog (secondary_sources) adds 100B-scale p99/QPS targets only—not a doc-count cold p50 curve.

sources_checked.finding: Vector Perf tab: only 10M × 1024 workload; URL query params do not expose alternate doc-count latencies
100B×1024×f16 (~200 TiB): p99≤200 ms, >1k QPS fleet targets; roofline ~100–10k QPS; no (N, cold p50) point — see secondary_sources[ann_v3_blog]

same_doc_count_different_path.note: Warm vs cold at same N is a cache-path comparison, not doc-count scaling
